Off Calendar Jun-21-2022 Continued To Jul-19-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Apr-22-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Robert Pollak, in person, for Juyeon Song and in propria persona, Co-Executors.

The court points counsel to the local rules regarding fees for paralegal work and the early distribution of funds that are not included in the accounting schedules. Counsel offers testimony in support of his request for paralegal fees and reports that the distributions were not included in the accounting because they had been made prior to the decedent's death. Counsel points the court to the documents he filed on Nov-21-2021, "Declaration of John Pollak" and "Executors Argument, etc." in support of his testimony regarding the distributions.

After additional discussion, the Accounting Petition is taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Gomez. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Mar-01-2022 Continued To Apr-19-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Pollak, in person, for Juyeon Song and in propria persona, Co-Executors.

The court notes the ongoing issues with the accounting and reminds counsel that by local rule, the accounting is to begin from date of death value which via the corrected Inventory and Appraisal filed should be $1,448,000. Mr. Pollak reports of issues obtaining bank records to verify the balances as requested by the court. Mr. Pollak confirms that he has not contacted anyone on the list provided to him by the court of individuals who may be able to assist with the accounting.

After additional discussion, the Accounting Petition is continued to Apr-19-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Cristobal. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Oct-12-2021 Continued To Nov-23-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Pollak appeared in person for the Co-Executors.

The court stated that what was submitted raises more questions than it provides answers and that it will not take oral explanations. Mr. Pollak stated that the original filing describes the property. The court explained to Mr. Pollak that he needs to file an inventory and appraisal. Mr. Pollak stated that he filed an inventory and appraisal without the attachment that shows the amount that was on deposit from the assets from the sale of a property, that the early distributions were made during the decedent's lifetime and that the money went to four parties. Mr. Pollak also stated that the decedent owned the real property only because it was an installment sale and he was in contract. The court informed Mr. Pollak that the property needs to be inventoried. Mr. Pollak stated that $50,000.00 went to him because he has been designing, permitting, constructing and supervising the work that is going on with the property, spoke of the condition of the property and what needed to be repaired, stated that he had been working with the decedent off and on for 30-years, and that this is something that was agreed to by the decedent. The court informed Mr. Pollak that the interest in the real property needs to in writing, that an inventory and appraisal is needed, and that the funds need to be in separate insured accounts. The court also noted the date of death and that transactions ocurred after the date of death. Mr. Pollak stated that these were monies that were paid during the decedent's lifetime ($400,000.00), that the money was given eight months before his death, that this was an installment sale and that the remaining amounts were paid after his death. The Amended First and Final Account, etc. is continued to November 23, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on filing of the inventory and appraisal, and status of the insured accounts are also set for November 23, 2021. The court informed Mr. Pollak that he has one shot of carrying this all out in a clear way to submit support and articulation of what went on.

Judge: Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Lee. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Sep-28-2021 Continued To Oct-12-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Pollak, in court, for Juyeon Song and in propria persona, Co-Executors.

Mr. Pollak begins by reporting that the implied individual accounts not included in the Inventory and Appraisal (I&A) were joint accounts passed-on via right of survivorship. The court then inquires regarding the more than $900,000. Mr. Pollak reports that money is proceeds from the sale of home prior to death. The court then asks why the property had not been included in the I & A. Mr. Pollak provides oral testimony regarding the preceding and at the end, queries the court whether the submission of the escrow documents may assist in clarifying the issues. The court advises Mr. Pollak that he should submit the escrow documents if that will help clarify the accounting numbers. Mr. Pollak is instructed to file a break-down of the more than $900,000 via closing statement and email a copy of the document to the assigned Examiner as soon as it is submitted for e-filing but by no later than Oct-06-2021.

After additional discussion, the Amended First and Final Account, etc., is continued to Oct-12-2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: C. Andam. Not reported.
